_meta.description: Hook / headline persuasion library for Choir. App-wide read-only seed data. Engineers load into global_library.hooks, global_library.emotional_hook_types, global_library.fill_in_templates, global_library.hook_prompts, global_library.headline_formulas. Members reference these by id; no member/org FK on this layer.
_meta.extraction_note: The 'LinkedIn Hooks Database CSV' cited in linkedin-hooks-playbook.md (141 hooks) is NOT materialized as rows in the KB. Only the category-count summary table + ~15 sample hook templates by category are rendered. Every CONCRETE hook string present in the four source files is extracted below verbatim. The 141 number is the documented category breakdown, not 141 retrievable strings. See unresolved_citations + net_new_flags. Counts: hooks[]=72 concrete entries, emotional_hook_types[]=12, fill_in_templates[]=10, hook_prompts[]=30, headline_formulas[]=34.

category_breakdown_141_db.note: Documented composition of the 141-hook database. The full per-row CSV was not exported into the KB; only counts + samples below.

categories.description: Personal takes, industry commentary, contrarian views
How-to frameworks, step-by-step processes, tool recommendations
Curated lists, tool roundups, reference collections
Learnings from experience, mistakes made, wisdom gained
What went wrong, common errors, anti-patterns
In-depth analysis of a specific example or campaign
Product-related hooks (use sparingly to avoid being salesy)

five_hook_rules.note: Validation gate every hook must pass before publish. Engineer: use as a checklist scorer on member-authored hooks.

rules.name: Valuable
rules.test: Promises something the reader genuinely wants; signals the post is worth their time.

rules.name: Actionable
rules.test: Hints the post contains something the reader can act on; promises a concrete takeaway.

rules.name: Efficient
rules.test: Short. Ideal hook is 6 to 8 words. Performs better on mobile.

rules.name: Enticing
rules.test: Creates an open loop (question, tension, curiosity gap) compelling a 'see more' click.

rules.name: Engaging
rules.test: Something the reader wants to comment on, share, or save; speaks to their situation/identity/aspirations.

emotional_hook_types.name: Inspiration
emotional_hook_types.mechanism: Show an aspirational outcome. The reader sees a result or lifestyle they desire and is drawn in by the possibility.
emotional_hook_types.when_to_use: Success stories, milestone posts, transformation narratives
emotional_hook_types.example: I went from 0 to 10,000 followers in 6 months.

emotional_hook_types.name: Fear
emotional_hook_types.reader_reaction: I don't want to lose that
emotional_hook_types.mechanism: Trigger loss aversion. Humans are more motivated to avoid losing something than to gain something of equal value.
emotional_hook_types.when_to_use: Warning posts, 'stop doing this' content, mistake-based posts
emotional_hook_types.example: Your LinkedIn profile is costing you clients.

emotional_hook_types.name: Curiosity
emotional_hook_types.mechanism: Create an information gap. Three subtypes: Shocking truth ('The truth about X that nobody talks about'); Promise value ('Here are 7 ways to do X better'); Strong words ('secret', 'hidden', 'unexpected').
emotional_hook_types.example: The #1 reason your LinkedIn posts fail (it's not your content).

emotional_hook_types.name: Credibility
emotional_hook_types.mechanism: Lead with a result, credential, or experience that signals authority. The reader trusts the content because the author has earned the right to teach.
emotional_hook_types.when_to_use: Framework posts, how-to content, case studies
emotional_hook_types.example: After writing 500+ LinkedIn posts, here's what actually works.

emotional_hook_types.name: Counter-Narrative
emotional_hook_types.reader_reaction: That's the opposite of what I believe
emotional_hook_types.mechanism: Challenge a widely held belief. When a hook contradicts what the reader assumes to be true, they need to read on to understand why.
emotional_hook_types.when_to_use: Contrarian takes, opinion pieces, industry challenges
emotional_hook_types.example: Posting every day on LinkedIn is a terrible strategy.

emotional_hook_types.name: Counter-Intuitive
emotional_hook_types.reader_reaction: I never thought of it like that
emotional_hook_types.mechanism: Present a perspective the reader has never considered. Unlike counter-narrative (challenges existing beliefs), counter-intuitive reveals a genuinely new angle.
emotional_hook_types.when_to_use: Reframing posts, unconventional advice, fresh takes
emotional_hook_types.example: The best LinkedIn posts don't teach anything.

emotional_hook_types.name: Eloquence
emotional_hook_types.mechanism: Crystallize a feeling or experience the reader has had but never articulated. The hook puts words to what they already know.
emotional_hook_types.when_to_use: Observation posts, career truths, relatable content
emotional_hook_types.example: Most meetings could have been a Slack message.

emotional_hook_types.name: Faces
emotional_hook_types.mechanism: Human faces are hardwired attention magnets. The brain processes faces before text. Posts with recognizable or expressive faces get more initial stops in the feed.
emotional_hook_types.when_to_use: Personal story posts, selfie + insight posts, team posts
emotional_hook_types.note: Applies more to image selection than text hooks, but text can reference specific people.

emotional_hook_types.name: Education
emotional_hook_types.mechanism: Signal that the post contains genuinely useful information. The reader recognizes the topic as relevant to their growth or work.
emotional_hook_types.when_to_use: How-to posts, framework posts, tool recommendations
emotional_hook_types.example: 7 ChatGPT prompts that save me 5 hours/week.

emotional_hook_types.name: Surprise
emotional_hook_types.mechanism: Lead with an unexpected data point, result, or fact. Surprise breaks the pattern of predictable content and forces the reader to engage.
emotional_hook_types.when_to_use: Data-driven posts, results posts, research-based content
emotional_hook_types.example: 48% of all LinkedIn posts are text + image.

emotional_hook_types.name: Celebration
emotional_hook_types.mechanism: Announce a win, milestone, or achievement. People are drawn to celebrate with others due to social bonding instincts.
emotional_hook_types.when_to_use: Fundraising announcements, new job posts, milestone celebrations
emotional_hook_types.example: We just closed our Series A.
emotional_hook_types.warning: Celebration hooks get high engagement but low saves. Use sparingly.

emotional_hook_types.name: Identity
emotional_hook_types.mechanism: Speak directly to a specific group. When readers see themselves described, they feel personally addressed.
emotional_hook_types.when_to_use: Niche-targeted content, role-specific advice, community posts
emotional_hook_types.example: If you're a solo founder doing your own marketing...

fill_in_templates.name: Dream Outcome Without Obstacle
fill_in_templates.example: How to grow on LinkedIn (without posting every day)
fill_in_templates.why_it_works: Removes the perceived barrier, making the goal feel more achievable.

fill_in_templates.name: Achievement in Steps
fill_in_templates.example: I built a 6-figure agency in 5 steps
fill_in_templates.why_it_works: Combines credibility (the achievement) with a promise of structure (the steps).

fill_in_templates.name: Things to Overcome and Achieve
fill_in_templates.example: 9 habits to stop procrastinating and double your output
fill_in_templates.why_it_works: Dual benefit structure increases perceived value.

fill_in_templates.name: Dream Outcome in Timeframe
fill_in_templates.format: This is how I [dream outcome] in [timeframe]
fill_in_templates.example: This is how I got 10K followers in 90 days

fill_in_templates.name: Bold Opinion
fill_in_templates.example: I don't care if you disagree -- cold outreach still works
fill_in_templates.why_it_works: Polarization drives engagement. People who agree feel validated; people who disagree feel compelled to comment.

fill_in_templates.name: Popular Opinion Challenge
fill_in_templates.example: Most marketers think content is king. They're wrong.
fill_in_templates.why_it_works: Counter-narrative hook that creates an open loop.

fill_in_templates.name: Dream Outcome + Solution
fill_in_templates.example: Want more inbound leads? Stop selling in your posts.
fill_in_templates.why_it_works: Starts with desire, delivers unexpected advice.

fill_in_templates.name: Risk Taken + Fortunate Outcome
fill_in_templates.format: [X] years ago I [risk taken], but [fortunate outcome]
fill_in_templates.example: 3 years ago I quit my $200K job. Best decision I ever made.
fill_in_templates.why_it_works: Story arc in one line. Risk + reward = compelling narrative.

fill_in_templates.name: Biggest Mistake
fill_in_templates.format: The biggest mistake I made as [job role]: [the mistake]
fill_in_templates.example: The biggest mistake I made as a founder: hiring too fast
fill_in_templates.why_it_works: Vulnerability + authority. People learn from mistakes more than successes.

fill_in_templates.name: Obstacle to Dream Outcome in Timeframe
fill_in_templates.format: How I went from [obstacle] to [dream outcome] in [timeframe]
fill_in_templates.example: How I went from 0 clients to fully booked in 6 months

hook_prompts.prompt: I [achievement] in [timeframe]. Here's how.
[X] years ago, I [starting point]. Today, I [current state].
The #1 thing that helped me [achievement].
I spent [time/money] learning [skill]. Here's what I found.
I studied [X] [things]. Here's what they had in common.
[X] lessons I learned from [experience/person].
I used to think [old belief]. I was wrong.
The hardest part of [role/activity] isn't what you think.
[Famous person] taught me [lesson]. Here's the story.
If I had to start [activity] from scratch, I'd do this.
[X] things I'd tell my younger self about [topic].
I failed at [thing] [X] times. Here's what finally worked.
The difference between [average] and [great] at [skill].
I made [amount] from [activity]. Here's the breakdown.
[X] tools I use every day that most people don't know about.
Unpopular opinion: [your take on industry topic].
Stop [common activity]. Start [better alternative].
[X]% of [group] get [topic] wrong. Here's why.
The [industry] is broken. Here's what needs to change.
You don't need [perceived requirement]. You need [actual requirement].
[Common advice] is the worst advice in [industry].
Everyone talks about [topic]. Nobody talks about [hidden truth].
[X] signs you're [negative state]. And how to fix it.
What [successful people] do differently about [topic].
The real reason [common problem] happens.
I asked [X] [experts/customers] one question. Here's what they said.
[Topic] is not about [common assumption]. It's about [reality].
The biggest lie in [industry] is [the lie].
[X] mistakes that are killing your [desired outcome].
Here's why [counterintuitive thing] actually works.
